---
title: "Mini Project 2: Explore & Interrogate"
subtitle: "If you want a subtitle, put it here"
author: John Helveston - Update with your name
date: September 05, 2026 - Update with your submission date
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: flatly
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
    warning = FALSE,
    message = FALSE,
    fig.path = "figs/", # Folder where rendered plots are saved
    fig.width = 7.252, # Default plot width
    fig.height = 4, # Default plot height
    fig.retina = 3, # For better plot resolution
    comment = "#>"
)

# Load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
```

<!-- Steps 1-3 (get organized, document the data, preview the data) happen
outside this file -- don't skip them: fill in data/README.md for BOTH data
files, and preview both before you start. -->

# Load the Data

<!-- Step 4: have Claude Code read in olympics_2016.csv and
population_2016.csv. Pause here and think about your strategy before
moving on -- don't let the agent start cleaning or joining yet. -->

# Strategy

<!-- Step 5: write your numbered strategy here, before any cleaning or
joining code. It must address: how you'll count medals correctly (not
just counting rows), how you'll handle team-number suffixes and
non-country teams, how you'll join to population (and handle name
mismatches), and what you'll check afterward. -->

# Direct the Agent

<!-- Step 6: have Claude Code implement your strategy step by step, editing
this file directly. Review the code and diffs as it goes -- don't just
accept the final result all at once. -->

# Interrogate

<!-- Step 7: document the specific checks you ran and what you found,
including anywhere Claude Code's first attempt was wrong and how you
caught it. Make sure you cover:
  - medal count check against the real, official Rio 2016 medal table
  - join check: did every medal-winning country find a population match?
  - the headline comparison: rank by raw medal count vs. rank by medals
    per capita -- show both and explain which countries move the most -->

# Explore Your Own Questions

<!-- Step 8: at least 2 more research questions, each using a genuinely
different chart type (ranking, comparison, distribution, relationship, or
composition -- no two questions from the same category). State each
question, make the chart, and write an interpretation. -->

# Document Your Process

<!-- Step 9: save your cleaned/joined data as my_clean_data.csv in the data
folder, and write your reflection here -- biggest challenges, where the
agent needed correcting, what you checked to be confident, what you'd do
differently. -->

<!-- Step 10: once you've rendered and proofread report.html, run /export
in Claude Code and save it as transcript.md in this folder before you zip
everything up for submission. -->
